ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Amaa o daa bi zaŋdi dari maa sɔŋdi biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ bɛn ka yaa la bɛn’ sani, ka di zuɣu daa chɛ ka Leeban biŋkɔbiri nyɛ zaɣ’ gbarima, ka Yaakubu dina nyɛ bɛn mali yaa,
translated: But he did not use the branches to help the weaker animals, only the stronger ones, so that Laban's animals became few, and Jacob's became strong,

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: ka o daa nya ba ka yɛli, “Naawuni sansani m-bɔŋɔ!” Dinzuɣu ka o daa boli nimaani yuli Mahanaim.
translated: and when he saw them, he said, “This is God’s camp!” So he called that place Mahanaim.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Naawuni daa nam ninsala ŋ-ŋmahindila omaŋa, ka o be kaman Naawuni ni be shɛm. O daa nam ba la doo mini paɣa.
translated: God created mankind in his own image, in the likeness of God he created them; male and female he created them.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Nuhu zuliya m-bɔŋɔ: Nuhu daa nyɛla wuntizɔra ŋun jɛmdi Naawuni, ka ka galimi o ʒiɛman’ ni.
translated: Nuhu's story: Nuhu was a righteous man who worshipped God and did not associate anything with Him.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli Ibrahimma, “Di simdi ni a min’ a zuliya maa gbibi n daalikauli maa bɛ ʒiɛmana zaa ni.
translated: And God said to Abraham, "As for you, you must keep my covenant, you and your descendants after you for the generations to come."

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Amaa o daa bi zaŋdi dari maa sɔŋdi biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ bɛn ka yaa la bɛn’ sani, ka di zuɣu daa chɛ ka Leeban biŋkɔbiri nyɛ zaɣ’ gbarima, ka Yaakubu dina nyɛ bɛn mali yaa,
translated: But he did not use the rods to help the weaker animals mate near the stronger ones, so Laban's animals remained weak, while Jacob's grew strong.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: ka o daa nya ba ka yɛli, “Naawuni sansani m-bɔŋɔ!” Dinzuɣu ka o daa boli nimaani yuli Mahanaim.
translated: and he saw them and said, "This is the camp of God!" Therefore he called that place Mahanaim.

ref: As the sign that you are keeping this promise, you must circumcise every man and boy in your family.
text: Gunimiya, ka di nyɛ m mini ya daalikauli maa shihirili.
translated: Truly, it is a covenant between me and you.

ref: I will always keep the promise I have made to you and your descendants, because I am your God and their God.
text: “Ka n ni chɛ ka m min’ a ni a zuliya mali alikauli din ni ʒiɛya sahakam bɛ ʒiɛmana ni. Ka n ni nyɛ a min’ a zuliya maa Naawuni.
translated: And I will establish my covenant between me and you and your descendants after you throughout their generations, for an everlasting covenant, to be God to you and to your descendants after you.

ref: For seven years Egypt will have more than enough grain,
text: Yuma ayopɔin din kanna puuni, bindira ni niŋ pam Ijipti tiŋgbɔŋ ŋɔ zaa ni.
translated: In the past seven years, there has been an abundance of food throughout this land of Egypt.

ref: where he cleaned out those wells that the Philistines had stopped up. Isaac also gave each of the wells the same name that Abraham had given to them.
text: Ka Yisahaku daa lan lab’ gbi kɔbil’ shɛŋa bɛ ni daa pun gbi o ba Ibrahimma ʒiɛman’ ni la, dama Filistianim’ n-daa ŋar’ li Ibrahimma kum nyaaŋa. Ka Yisahaku daa zaŋ o ba ni daa zaŋ yu’ shɛŋa bol’ kɔbilisi maa la n-lan bol’ li.
translated: And Isaac dug again the wells of water which they had dug in the days of his father Abraham, for the Philistines had stopped them up after the death of Abraham. And Isaac gave them the same names which his father had given them.

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: N ni chaŋ nimaani ti nya bɛ shiri tumdi bɛ ni yɛri ni bɛ tumdi shɛm maa zaa bee bɛ bi tumdi li.”
translated: I will go there and see whether they are truly doing everything they say they are doing or they are not doing it.
